title: Risk vs. Ambiguity: Two Kinds of Uncertainty

runtime.expected_outcome: EFE decomposes into risk (expected KL from preferences) and ambiguity (expected conditional entropy). Plotting both shows the agent prioritising disambiguation early.

symbols.risk: divergence between predicted outcomes and preferences
symbols.ambiguity: expected conditional entropy; reducible by disambiguating observations

audiences.kid: There's 'I know what's coming and I don't like it' and there's 'I can't tell what's coming at all'. Both are uncertainty. The agent treats them differently.
audiences.real: EFE = risk + ambiguity. Risk = divergence between predicted obs and preferences. Ambiguity = conditional entropy of obs given state. Disambiguating actions minimise the second term even without preference pull.
audiences.equation: Risk = D_KL[q(o|pi) || p(o)]; ambiguity = E_q[H[p(o|s)]]. Both are minimised by good policies; their mix characterises the agent's style.
audiences.derivation: Starting from G = E_q[ln q(s|pi) - ln p(o,s)], adding and subtracting ln p(o) splits G into the KL-to-preferences and the expected conditional entropy (Schwartenbeck et al. 2019, eq 5).

orchestrate.objective: Decompose EFE into risk and ambiguity and show when each dominates.
orchestrate.context: deceptive_dead_end where initial ambiguity is high; watch the agent disambiguate before committing.

credits: Parr, Pezzulo & Friston (2022), Active Inference, MIT Press (CC BY-NC-ND), Ch 4.
Schwartenbeck, Passecker, Hauser, FitzGerald, Kronbichler & Friston (2019).
